op.name: split
op.notes: Split array into sub-arrays. Cost: numel(output).
op.summary: Split an array into multiple sub-arrays as views into `ary`.

blocks.text: Split an array into multiple sub-arrays as views into `ary`.

desc_blocks.text: Array to be divided into sub-arrays.

If `indices_or_sections` is an integer, N, the array will be divided
into N equal arrays along `axis`.  If such a split is not possible,
an error is raised.

If `indices_or_sections` is a 1-D array of sorted integers, the entries
indicate where along `axis` the array is split.  For example,
``[2, 3]`` would, for ``axis=0``, result in

If an index exceeds the dimension of the array along `axis`,
an empty sub-array is returned correspondingly.
The axis along which to split, default is 0.
A list of sub-arrays as views into `ary`.

blocks.code:
>>> import numpy as np
>>> x = np.arange(9.0)
>>> np.split(x, 3)
[array([0.,  1.,  2.]), array([3.,  4.,  5.]), array([6.,  7.,  8.])]

>>> x = np.arange(8.0)
>>> np.split(x, [3, 5, 6, 10])
[array([0.,  1.,  2.]),
 array([3.,  4.]),
 array([5.]),
 array([6.,  7.]),
 array([], dtype=float64)]

blocks.text: array_split — Split an array into multiple sub-arrays of equal or near-equal size.  Does not raise an exception if an equal division cannot be made.
hsplit — Split array into multiple sub-arrays horizontally (column-wise).
vsplit — Split array into multiple sub-arrays vertically (row wise).
dsplit — Split array into multiple sub-arrays along the 3rd axis (depth).
concatenate — Join a sequence of arrays along an existing axis.
stack — Join a sequence of arrays along a new axis.
hstack — Stack arrays in sequence horizontally (column wise).
vstack — Stack arrays in sequence vertically (row wise).
dstack — Stack arrays in sequence depth wise (along third dimension).
